display.purpose: Hero verdict and the big risk score. Pixelify Sans, the loudest the machine gets.

headline.purpose: Scanned project name on the report. Pixelify Sans.

title.purpose: Section headings, feature titles, button text. Pixelify Sans bold.

body.purpose: Descriptions, summaries, TL;DR, red-flag copy. Space Grotesk at Ink, 65-75ch.

label.purpose: Arcade-bezel kickers and status tags. Press Start 2P uppercase, floored at ~10px.

mono.purpose: Target URLs, the copyable deep-dive prompt, the terminal search input.

shadows.name: cyan-glow
shadows.purpose: Default 'this box is alive' treatment on primary panels and the search bar.

shadows.name: green-glow
shadows.purpose: Positive-signal panels and safe-state pills announce their verdict through their glow.

shadows.name: text-bloom-cyan
shadows.purpose: Phosphor bleed on headings, scores, and the wordmark. Reduced to a single 7px shadow on mobile.

shadows.name: cta-cast
shadows.purpose: The SCAN button throwing cyan light onto the glass; climbs to 0 0 25px rgba(34,211,238,0.6) on hover.

motion.name: hover-transition
motion.purpose: Default state transition on borders, background washes, and text color (150-250ms band).

motion.name: blink
motion.purpose: Cursor / liveness dot and 'SCANNING TARGET...' status. Gated behind prefers-reduced-motion.

motion.name: neon-flicker
motion.purpose: The hero word 'Verify.' flickers like a failing tube. Gated (violates WCAG 2.3.1 flashing if ungated).

motion.name: breathe
motion.purpose: Gentle opacity pulse for a subtle alive feel. Gated behind prefers-reduced-motion.

motion.name: report-reveal
motion.purpose: Report content enters once on load (tailwindcss-animate). Not per-section choreography.

components.name: Primary Button (SCAN)
components.description: The one true CTA. Cyan fill, pixel bold, throws a cyan cast that intensifies on hover. One per screen.
components.html: <button class='ds-btn-scan'><svg width='16' height='16' viewBox='0 0 24 24' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2' stroke-linecap='round' stroke-linejoin='round'><circle cx='11' cy='11' r='8'/><path d='m21 21-4.3-4.3'/></svg>SCAN</button>
components.css: .ds-btn-scan { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:.5rem; height:3rem; padding:0 1.5rem; background:#06b6d4; color:#050a14; border:0; border-radius:0; font-family:'Pixelify Sans',monospace; font-weight:700; font-size:.875rem; box-shadow:0 0 15px rgba(34,211,238,.4); transition:all .2s; cursor:pointer; } .ds-btn-scan:hover { background:#22d3ee; box-shadow:0 0 25px rgba(34,211,238,.6); } .ds-btn-scan:focus-visible { outline:2px solid #0df2db; outline-offset:2px; }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Secondary / nav actions. Transparent on a low-opacity cyan stroke; border and wash climb on hover.
components.html: <button class='ds-btn-outline'><svg width='12' height='12' viewBox='0 0 24 24' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2' stroke-linecap='round' stroke-linejoin='round'><path d='M15 3h4a2 2 0 0 1 2 2v14a2 2 0 0 1-2 2h-4'/><polyline points='10 17 15 12 10 7'/><line x1='15' x2='3' y1='12' y2='12'/></svg>SIGN IN</button>
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:.375rem; height:2.75rem; padding:0 1rem; background:transparent; border:2px solid rgba(34,211,238,.3); border-radius:0; color:#22d3ee; font-family:'Pixelify Sans',monospace; font-weight:700; font-size:.75rem; transition:all .2s; cursor:pointer; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background:rgba(34,211,238,.1); border-color:rgba(34,211,238,.6); color:#67e8f9; } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline:2px solid #0df2db; outline-offset:2px; }

components.name: Search Input
components.description: The container is the input: a glowing cyan border box wrapping a transparent, border-less terminal field.
components.css: .ds-input-search { display:flex; align-items:center; padding:.5rem; border:2px solid rgba(34,211,238,.25); background:rgba(10,17,31,.5); box-shadow:0 0 5px rgba(34,211,238,.3), 0 0 15px rgba(34,211,238,.15), inset 0 0 10px rgba(34,211,238,.05); } .ds-input-search input { flex:1; height:3rem; padding:0 .75rem; background:transparent; border:0; outline:0; color:#d7e8ea; font-family:ui-monospace,monospace; font-size:.875rem; } .ds-input-search input::placeholder { color:rgba(34,211,238,.3); }

components.name: Pixel Score Gauge
components.description: The signature object. A large pixel score in the risk-spectrum color above a ten-segment HP bar. Shown here as a low-risk 82/100.
components.html:
82
/100
components.css: .ds-score-gauge { display:inline-flex; flex-direction:column; align-items:center; gap:.25rem; } .ds-score-num { font-family:'Pixelify Sans',monospace; font-weight:700; font-size:3rem; line-height:1; color:#10b981; text-shadow:0 0 7px rgba(16,185,129,.7), 0 0 20px rgba(16,185,129,.4); } .ds-score-bar { display:flex; gap:2px; } .ds-score-bar span { width:8px; height:12px; } .ds-score-bar .on { background:#10b981; } .ds-score-bar .off { background:rgba(255,255,255,.06); } .ds-score-max { font-family:'Press Start 2P',monospace; font-size:.625rem; color:#6c8093; }

components.name: Danger Panel
components.description: A semantic container whose border, glow, and heading all carry the verdict color. Shown here as a red-flag panel; swap pink for green/cyan/yellow by meaning.
components.html:
RED FLAGS (2)

Anonymous team with no multisig. Liquidity is unlocked and can be pulled at any time.
components.css: .ds-panel-danger { border:2px solid rgba(255,45,120,.25); background:rgba(255,45,120,.03); padding:1.25rem; box-shadow:0 0 5px rgba(255,45,120,.3), 0 0 15px rgba(255,45,120,.15), inset 0 0 10px rgba(255,45,120,.05); } .ds-panel-head { display:flex; align-items:center; gap:.5rem; margin-bottom:.75rem; font-family:'Pixelify Sans',monospace; font-weight:700; font-size:.875rem; color:#ff2d78; text-shadow:0 0 7px rgba(255,45,120,.7); } .ds-panel-danger p { margin:0; color:#d7e8ea; font-family:'Space Grotesk',sans-serif; font-size:.875rem; line-height:1.6; }

components.name: Status Pill
components.description: A sharp hand-rolled tag whose color IS its meaning. Shown as the safe-green UNLIMITED state.
components.css: .ds-pill-status { display:inline-block; padding:.375rem .75rem; border:2px solid rgba(16,185,129,.2); background:rgba(16,185,129,.1); color:#10b981; font-family:'Press Start 2P',monospace; font-size:.625rem; letter-spacing:.08em; box-shadow:0 0 5px rgba(16,185,129,.3), 0 0 15px rgba(16,185,129,.15), inset 0 0 10px rgba(16,185,129,.05); }

narrative.overview: IsThisSafeToApe is a neon coin-op machine that tells you whether to ape. You feed it a URL like you'd feed it a quarter; it renders a verdict in Pixelify Sans, drops your risk as a ten-segment HP bar, and glows at you from a dark cabinet. The whole surface is a CRT: scanlines wash over near-black glass, cyan phosphor bleeds off the edges of text, and every label reads like it was silkscreened onto an arcade bezel. Playful degen on the surface, dead-serious risk analysis underneath. Density is deliberate and vertical: a single centered column, generous breathing room, no side rails or dashboards. The machine answers fast — score first, verdict second, receipts on demand. Color is never decoration; it is the verdict itself, mapped from a green-to-pink risk spectrum a degen reads before a single word. It rejects enterprise fintech / compliance SaaS, cute / pastel web3, and AI slop alike.
keyCharacteristics: Arcade-cabinet dark: near-black #050a14 glass, cyan phosphor glow, CRT scanline overlay.
Sharp everywhere: zero border radius. Corners are pixels, not pills.
Color as verdict: a green-yellow-orange-pink risk spectrum that carries meaning, never mood.
Pixel identity, readable body: Pixelify Sans for the machine's voice; Space Grotesk for anything a human actually reads.
Glow, not shadow: depth comes from neon bloom and border intensity, never drop shadows.
Readability wins every conflict (the spine of this system).

rules.name: The Risk-Color Rule
rules.body: The green-yellow-orange-pink spectrum means exactly one thing: verdict severity. Never use a spectrum color for decoration, mood, or brand flourish. If pink appears, something is dangerous. If a screen wants a warm accent, it uses Neon Cyan, not the spectrum.

rules.name: The One-Cyan Rule
rules.body: Three cyans drift across the app — Neon Cyan (#22d3ee, canonical brand), Cyan Action (#06b6d4, the CTA fill), and Primary Teal (#0df2db, the inherited focus-ring token). Do not introduce a fourth. When two would read as the same cyan, converge on Neon Cyan.

rules.name: The Readable-Ink Rule
rules.body: Reading text is Ink (#d7e8ea) or, at most, Ink Muted (#6c8093) at full opacity. Gray-on-neon and low-opacity muted text on Void fail contrast; if body copy is even close to 4.5:1, it goes to Ink.

rules.name: The Pixel-Display Rule
rules.body: Pixelify Sans and Press Start 2P carry identity — headings, scores, wordmark, kickers. The moment text exists to be read rather than recognized, it is Space Grotesk. Pixel fonts never carry a paragraph.

rules.name: The Readable-Floor Rule
rules.body: Readability wins every conflict with the theme. Press Start 2P is near-unreadable below ~10px, yet the current code ships text-[6px] and text-[7px] labels — that is readability debt, not a target. New work never goes below ~10px on pixel labels, never puts Ink Muted below full opacity for text, and never sacrifices a score, verdict, or sentence to the retro skin.

rules.name: The Glow-Not-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Depth is light, never darkness. If an element needs to feel raised or active, it glows brighter or its border climbs in opacity (border-cyan-500/15 to /40 on hover) — it never drops a gray shadow. A gray box-shadow anywhere is a bug: it reads as a 2014 web app pasted onto an arcade cabinet.

dos: Do answer with the score first. Verdict (score + risk label + TL;DR) reads above the fold; the six-dimension breakdown and deep-dive prompt are the receipts below.
Do let color carry the verdict — green-yellow-orange-pink for severity, Neon Cyan for the machine's voice, and nothing decorative from the spectrum.
Do keep corners sharp (rounded-none) and depth made of glow (neon-box-*, border-opacity climbs on hover).
Do set body copy in Space Grotesk at Ink (#d7e8ea); reserve Pixelify Sans / Press Start 2P for identity, scores, and kickers.
Do hold pixel labels at ~10px or larger and keep every animation gated behind prefers-reduced-motion (blink, neon-flicker, breathe).
Do use full border-2 borders and semantic panel colors as the container vocabulary; one flat panel, never nested.
donts: Don't drift toward enterprise fintech / compliance SaaS — no navy-and-white, no sterile trust badges, no KYC-gate chrome. This is a tool for people already in the arena.
Don't go cute / pastel web3 — no rounded bubbles, soft pastels, friendly mascots, or earnest onboarding. The humor has teeth.

Don't ship AI slop: no gradient text (background-clip: text), no identical icon-card grids, no glassmorphism-by-default, no tracked-uppercase eyebrow above every section as reflex scaffolding. The pixel kicker is a deliberate named system — that is the allowed exception, not license to eyebrow everything.
Don't ship text-[6px] / text-[7px] pixel labels or Ink-Muted text below full opacity — the current ones are debt to pay down, not a pattern to copy. Readability wins every conflict with the theme.
Don't add a gray box-shadow anywhere. Depth is light; a dark shadow reads as a 2014 app glued onto the cabinet.
Don't introduce a fourth cyan, and don't use a risk-spectrum color as a mood accent. If pink is on screen, something is dangerous.
